text:
The × Guard's × Duty (バンニン×ノ×セキニン, Ban'nin × No × Sakinin) is the 23 rd episode of the Hunter × Hunter 2011 series. It first aired on March 18 th , 2012.

Overview

After successfully opening the Testing Gate on their own Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio continue their way to find Killua, but then are stopped by another servant.

Summary

At Kukuroo Mountain, Zebro asks Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio how long they plan to stay. Gon responds firmly, stating that they won’t leave until they meet Killua. Once the group finishes their duties, Zebro leads them to the servants' quarters, introducing them to a fellow worker named Seaquant. With a cautious tone, Seaquant invites them to make themselves comfortable, followed by a subtle warning in his words.

As they settle in, Seaquant scoffs at their goal of meeting Killua, suggesting their efforts are pointless. Leorio, quickly losing patience, fires back, insisting that they mean every word. However, Seaquant remains unbothered. With grim certainty, he advises them to give up and return home, warning of the dangers beyond the Testing Gate and the grueling path that lies ahead.

Gon steps forward, confidently assuring Seaquant that they are fully aware of the inherent risks but remain steadfast in their resolve to proceed. He articulates his desire to make amends for not being there when Killua was in trouble, emphasizing the depth of their friendship. In response to Gon’s intent, Zebro invites them to train at their place, proposing that the three of them could combine their efforts to open the Testing Gate. Despite Gon’s aversion to being tested, the trio ultimately agrees to accept Zebro’s offer.

Zebro equips each of them with a fifty-kilogram vest, instructing them to wear it at all times except during sleep. The trio embarks on their training while clad in the vests, facing various challenges, such as drinking from cups that weigh twenty kilograms. Zebro elaborates that everything within the quarters shares this peculiar weight. Seaquant, convinced they will soon surrender, derides their efforts, which provokes irritation in Leorio. As Seaquant departs, Zebro offers his apologies to the group for his colleague's demeanor, assuring them that Seaquant is not a bad person at heart.

Within the confines of the mansion, Kikyo and Kalluto enter a room, revealing Killua restrained in handcuffs, his body bearing the cruel marks of a whip as Milluki continues his torment. Milluki informs Kikyo that Killua remains obstinately unrepentant, prompting her to plead with Killua to comply. In a surge of anger, Killua shouts at her, but Milluki swiftly retaliates, lashing him with the whip and warning him not to raise his voice at their mother. Kikyo then commands Milluki to halt his punishment.

Milluki remarks that Killua’s friend Gon will attempt to open the Testing Gate, to which Kikyo confidently asserts that he will fail. However, now aware of Gon’s presence, Killua responds with conviction, stating that Gon will succeed and go even further beyond the gate.

Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio stand before the Testing Gate as Zebro and Seaquant watch closely. Just as Gon is about to test the door, Leorio and Kurapika step forward, urging him to take a break and allow his injured hand to heal while they attempt their own turn. Resolute, Leorio, and Kurapika press against the door with all their might, but it won’t budge.

Afterward, Kurapika and Leorio engage in various training exercises, each striving to enhance their physical prowess. Although Gon is sidelined by his injury, he remains determined. He quietly sneaks into practice whenever the others are asleep or unaware of his actions, driven by an unwavering desire to improve.

As days go by, their efforts continue to be in vain. Seaquant urges them to abandon their efforts. Leorio snaps back, telling Seaquant to stop watching if he cannot bear to see them succeed. Kurapika interjects, explaining that they are determined to help Gon reach Killua, so they will keep pushing forward. Seaquant unexpectedly alters his approach and begins to provide them with tips on opening the door as he walks away. Taking Seaquant's advice to heart, the two successfully make the door budge, much to their astonishment. However, the door quickly rebounds, causing them to lose their balance; despite this setback, they feel a sense of accomplishment in their progress.

Meanwhile, Seaquant updates Gotoh on the presence of their visitors.

Kurapika and Leorio are set to test the door again, and Zebro suggests they remove their vests to facilitate the task. With this strategic alteration, Leorio feels a surge of confidence, believing that their efforts will be considerably more successful. They begin to push, creating a small opening, but the door remains immobile. Suddenly, Gon removes the cloth that supports his injured hand and joins in the effort. When Leorio and Kurapika question him about his injury, he reassures them that it feels significantly better now. United in purpose, the three of them exert their collective strength against the door, and through their combined determination, they finally succeed in forcing it open.

Zebro and Seaquant gesture toward the direction of the mountain, with Zebro admitting that, despite his two decades of service to the family, he harbors a profound sense of shame for never having ventured there. With that, Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio exchange their farewells.

As they progress on their journey, Zebro believes that the trio will prevail, whereas Seaquant expresses skepticism. He reflects on an incident from three years prior, when an apprentice butler managed to fend off him and his allies, noting that the Zoldycks and their staff are monsters.

In a gazebo on the mountain, Gotoh briefs Kikyo on Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio's progress, particularly emphasizing their recent success in overcoming the Testing Door. Concerned, Kikyo commands Gotoh to prevent the group from advancing further, asserting that she does not want them anywhere near her son.

On their way to Killua, a butler named Canary stands in the group's path. With an uninviting tone, she instructs them to leave the premises, asserting that they are trespassing and cannot pass without permission. Gon quickly informs Canary that they called ahead and have entered the Testing Gate. However, Canary points out that they lack the necessary permission from the butlers.

Gon then asks how they can obtain a permit, emphasizing that they are friends of Killua. In response, Canary replies that she does not know, as no one has ever secured a permit. Undeterred, Gon insists that they will proceed, even without the required authorization.
Canary draws a definitive line, warning that anyone who dares to cross it will face her wrath. As Leorio and Kurapika prepare to act, Gon signals them to hold their positions. He confidently strides toward Canary, stepping over the line. She swiftly brandishes her weapon in retaliation, striking Gon and sending him reeling backward. This action prompts Kurapika and Leorio to ready their weapons. Still, before they can launch an attack, Gon intervenes, commanding them not to interfere, insisting that he will manage the situation independently.

While on the ground, Gon tells Canary they do not intend to fight her; they merely seek to see Killua. However, Canary clarifies that she has no interest in their motives, insisting that she is simply following orders. Rising to his feet once again, Gon attempts to cross the line, but Canary retaliates, forcefully sending him sprawling back to the ground. Meanwhile, Kalluto hides in the bushes, silently observing the confrontation. Canary repeats her warning that she will not allow them to pass. Undaunted, Gon slowly rises and declares his determination to see Killua. Hearing his words, Canary experiences a brief flashback, recalling the moment she greeted Killua upon his return home. As Killua walks past her in a defeated state, he utters words in a hushed tone, apparently conveying to Canary that he has made a friend. After a short pause, Gon steadily approaches Canary again, only to be struck again. The same cycle continues as the day wears on.

Gon, visibly beaten with one eye swollen shut, persists and again approaches Canary. Losing her composure, Canary angrily shouts at Gon to stop and strikes him again. Infuriated, she demands to know why Kurapika and Leorio aren’t doing anything to intervene. Both Leorio and Kurapika regard her with serious expressions, which only serves to unsettle Canary further.

Undeterred, Gon steps closer to her, eager to see his friend. Suddenly, he throws a punch that shatters a nearby block. For a moment, Canary is frozen, surprised by Gon’s determination. He reminds Canaryr that he crossed the line but asks why she isn’t hitting him, leaving her confused. He tells her she is not like Mike, suggesting that she has a heart even though she tries to hide her feelings. Gon also notices that when he mentions Killua, her eyes soften with a calm expression. Hearing this, Canary lowers her guard, tears streaming down her face. She pleads with the group to help Killua. Suddenly, a bullet strikes her in the head, causing her to collapse unconscious. Standing off to the side, Kikyo fires the shot in disappointment at her actions and then relays a message from Killua, stating that he does not wish to see them.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Trivia

Kurapika and Leorio didn't try to open the Testing Gate at the same time. Instead, both Gon and Kurapika managed to open one gate each, while Leorio even managed to open two.
Kikyo and Kalluto Zoldyck didn't appear before Kikyo knocked Canary in the manga. But Kalluto was seen hiding in the bushes before his mother shows up.
An added scene showed Kikyo and Kalluto going to Killua to inform him about his friends.
When Kikyo knocked Canary out, the projectile came from the tip of her fan. This was not seen in the manga.
